fields.caption: fila
fields.tableau_formula:
case [letter]
    WHEN 'a' THEN 1
    WHEN 'b' THEN 1
    WHEN 'c' THEN 1
    WHEN 'd' THEN 1
    WHEN 'e' THEN 1
    WHEN 'f' THEN 1
    WHEN 'g' THEN 2
    WHEN 'h' THEN 2
    WHEN 'i' THEN 2
    WHEN 'j' THEN 2
    WHEN 'k' THEN 2
    WHEN 'f' THEN 2
    WHEN 'h' THEN 3
    WHEN 'i' THEN 3
    WHEN 'j' THEN 3
    WHEN 'k' THEN 3
    WHEN 'l' THEN 3
    WHEN 'm' THEN 3
    WHEN 'n' THEN 3
    
END

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: Adaptation text
fields.tableau_formula:
IF ISNULL([initiative]) then ''
ELSE [NAME]+" "+([initiative])+" has participated in the following recent EU-funded projects on adaptation: "+ [EU_project]  END

fields.caption: Dynamic parragraph
fields.tableau_formula:
'According to RAMSES project (l) the city is in cluster ' + 
str(ATTR([RAMSES_cluster])) + ' ' + 
ATTR([RAMSES_description]) +  "
" + "
" + 'According to the RESIN project (m), the city belongs to the group ' 
+ ATTR([RESIN_group_name]) 
+ ', which includes ' + ATTR([RESIN_group_description]) + '.' + "
" + "
" + 'Cities of similar size '  + 
ATTR([population_brackets]) + ' in the group ' +
ATTR([RESIN_group_name]) + ' '+ ' are ' +
ATTR([cities_in_group]) + '.'

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: typePeriod
fields.tableau_formula:
IF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'aseline') THEN 'Baseline'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'uture') THEN 'Future'
END

fields.caption: typeLandUses
fields.tableau_formula:
IF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'UMZ') THEN 'UMZ'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Transport') THEN 'Transport'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Residential') THEN 'Residential'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Commercial') THEN 'Commercial'
END

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection
extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: typeMeasure
fields.tableau_formula:
IF LEFT([Pivot Field Names],3) = 'UMZ' THEN 'UMZ' 
ELSEIF  LEFT([Pivot Field Names],4) = 'Seal' THEN 'Seal' 
ELSE
    'Unknown'
END

fields.caption: year
fields.tableau_formula:
DATE(DATEPARSE ( "yyyy", IF [Calculation_8817203660295704576] = 'Seal' THEN
    RIGHT([Pivot Field Names],4)
ELSEIF (CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'_06')) THEN '2006'
ELSEIF (CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'_09')) THEN '2009'
ELSEIF (CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'_12')) THEN '2012'
ELSEIF (CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'_15')) THEN '2015'
END ))

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: Superscritps
fields.tableau_formula:
If Contains([Name],"Hot summer") then "a" 
ELSEIF Contains([Name],"Combined summer") then "a"
ELSEIF Contains([Name],"Cooling degree") then "a"  
ELSEIF Contains([Unit],"in 33") then "b" 
ELSEIF Contains([Name],"forest") then "c" 
ELSEIF Contains([Unit],"mm/decade") then "d" 
ELSEIF Contains([Unit],"%") then "e" 
ELSEIF Contains([Unit],"events/decade") then "f" 
ELSEIF Contains([Unit],"Months/30-") then "e" 
end

connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel-direct'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

fields.caption: Socio-economic and demographic characteristics (2014)(k)
fields.tableau_formula:
IF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Over') THEN 'OVER75'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Under') THEN 'UNDER5'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Foreig') THEN 'FOREIGN'
//ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Foreign Rank') THEN 'FOREIGN'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Lone Pens') THEN 'LONEPENS'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Lone Parent') THEN 'LONEPARENTS'
//ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Unemployment') THEN 'UNEMPLOYMENT'
ELSEIF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Unemploy') THEN 'UNEMPLOYMENT'
END

fields.caption: typeValueComun

aliases.Rank: Position in relation to other Urban Audit cities in Europe

fields.tableau_formula:
IF CONTAINS([Pivot Field Names],'Rank') THEN 'Rank'
ELSE 'Value*'
END

The M parameter 'DataFolder' is hardcoded to an absolute local path so the model only refreshes on the machine that built it. Every user must update this parameter (Transform data > Manage Parameters > DataFolder) to their own local migrations/eea-urban-adaptation/data/ path after cloning. Phase-2 fix: repoint to a Fabric Lakehouse/OneLake path (or a shared network/blob location) before handing the model to anyone else or deploying to a workspace.

5 'City_filter' calculated fields (parameter-equality idiom, IF [Parameters].[Parameter 1]=[NAME] THEN [NAME] END) across UA Cities, Land Use Flood Pct, Sealed Surface Pct, Climate Indicators, Social Vulnerability were deliberately NOT materialized as TMDL columns/measures. The underlying Name/City dimension column already carries the same values; the report-builder step should bind a native Power BI slicer/filter to that column instead of recreating this idiom.

2 ':Measure Names' pseudo-fields (UA Cities, Climate Indicators/table1) are Tableau-only virtual dimensions with no backing data in the extracted CSVs. Not materialized - Power BI has no direct equivalent; if a 'measure swap' style visual is needed, the report-builder will need to construct it manually (e.g. a disconnected parameter table + SWITCH measure).

'Letter_And_Words (group)' in Web References is a Tableau Group field. The parser does not capture Tableau group-membership mappings (which raw values roll up into which group), so it was approximated as a passthrough duplicate of 'Letter_And_Words' rather than a true grouped dimension. If the actual grouping is customer-visible/important, it must be re-created by hand from the source .twb.

'Pivot Field Names'/'Pivot Field Values' (Tableau's auto-generated pivot columns) are present in the extracted CSVs and referenced by several calculated-field formulas, but are missing from the parsed field enumeration in migration-spec.json for 4 of 5 pivoted data sources: perclandusesumz (both missing), able_pivot_2 (both missing), percsealedsurface (both missing), socdecvulnerability ('Pivot Field Names' missing, 'Pivot Field Values' present). Materialized as base TMDL columns anyway in all 4 tables since the model would otherwise be structurally broken (typePeriod/typeLandUses/Sector/Flood/Base_future/typeMeasure/year/the vulnerability-characteristic column all parse them). Parser gap to fix upstream so future migrations don't require this manual recovery.

'Code' and 'Unit' columns exist in the table1/Climate Indicators extracted CSV but are entirely absent from the parsed spec's field list. 'Unit' is directly required by the Superscritps and 'Climate indicators' calculated-field formulas (Contains([Unit],...)) - without materializing it those two calculated columns could not be reproduced at all. Added both as base TMDL columns; this is a critical correctness fix, not a nice-to-have, and should be raised as a parser bug.

'URL' column exists in the webreferences extracted CSV but is entirely absent from the parsed spec's field list (which only enumerates letter, Word, Letter_And_Words, fila, Letter_And_Words (group), Number of Records - 6 fields). Not referenced by any calculated field, but materialized as a base column anyway since it is a genuinely useful footnote-hyperlink column for the report.

The 5 gauge worksheets (ws.cdd_0_1, ws.obs_dr_0_1_ok, ws.prec_obs_win_0_1_ok, ws.ssr_0_1_ok, ws.green_0_1_ok) plot a 0-1-scaled column (e.g. CDD_0_1) against reference lines on a -5..105 axis. The original Tableau axis calculation that reconciled this scale mismatch could not be resolved from the spec (referenced only as an UNRESOLVED:usr:Calculation_...:qk placeholder). Created explicit '<Base> ' value measures that do SUM(column)*100 to align with the reference-line scale - a well-reasoned inference from the data, not a verified fact. Recommend confirming against the live Tableau workbook/gauge visual before the customer demo if time allows.

ws.green_0_1_ok has Min/Max reference lines in the source but no Average/Target reference line. 'Green 0 1 Target' was set to a fallback value of 50 (the same pattern used by the other 4 gauges' midpoint), which is an approximation, not sourced from actual spec/worksheet data. Flag to the customer as an open question - what should this gauge's target actually be?

The parsed spec has zero populated 'formatting.number_format' entries anywhere in the workbook, so all TMDL formatString values were assigned heuristically (percent-scaled-0-100 fields get '0.00\%', 0-1 fraction fields get '0.0000%', rank/code/id-like integers get summarizeBy:none with plain formatting, everything else defaults to plain numeric). None of these were verified against the original Tableau worksheet formatting - a visual side-by-side comparison with the live workbook is recommended before the demo for any number that will be shown on-screen.

Modeling best practice generally recommends Decimal (fixed 4-decimal-digit) over Double for Power BI columns. This model deliberately uses 'double' for the ~90 real-valued climate/statistical indicator columns in UA Cities/Climate Indicators/etc. because Decimal's fixed precision would visibly truncate genuine high-precision scientific values (e.g. 0.460384607315). This is an intentional, documented deviation from the general guideline, not an oversight.

Per modeling best practice, every aggregatable numeric column should ideally be hidden behind an explicit measure. Given the task's explicit scope (34 calculated fields + 15 gauge-support measures) and time constraints, explicit measures were created ONLY for the in-scope calculated fields and the gauge family - the ~90 other numeric indicator columns (e.g. drought_rcp45_2041, SSR_2071_2100, perc_green, etc.) remain visible with default implicit SUM/AVG aggregation available in Desktop's field well, rather than each getting a named explicit measure. This is a scope-management decision, flagged for a possible phase-2 pass if the customer wants a fully 'measures-only' field list.

Several Tableau fields carry display-value aliases (e.g. socdecvulnerability's characteristic codes OVER75->'People 75 years old or older [%]', typePeriod's 'Baseline'->'Baseline (1961-1990)', typeValueComun's 'Value*'->'Value'). These alias mappings were intentionally NOT baked into the physical column values, because at least one worksheet filter (ws.3_land_uses_umz_potencially) filters on the raw code, so raw codes had to be preserved. The alias text is documented here for the report-builder to apply as legend/tooltip display text (e.g. via a mapping table, 'Display Value' calculated column, or visual-level renaming) if the customer wants the friendlier labels shown.

The general reshape_hint:pivot_derived guidance calls for Table.UnpivotOtherColumns in Power Query. In this workbook, the extracted CSVs already reflect Tableau's own post-pivot long/narrow shape (Pivot Field Names/Values columns already exist per row) because the .hyper extract was written after Tableau's data-source-level pivot was applied. So the 11 pivot_derived fields only needed Power Query *conditional columns* (parsing the already-narrow Pivot Field Names text) - not an actual unpivot step. This is a beneficial deviation from the generic guidance, correctly matching this specific data shape, but should be re-verified per-workbook on future migrations since it will not always hold.

No Fabric workspace, Power BI Desktop instance, or MCP server was available in this environment, so the entire TMDL/PBIP structure (database.tmdl, model.tmdl, relationships.tmdl, all 7 table files) was hand-authored against documented conventions (references/TMDL.md, references/pbip.md) and could not be round-tripped through a live Desktop open or a DAX EVALUATE query. Syntax was reviewed carefully (measure/column ordering, quoting of names with spaces, M partition logic) but this is NOT a substitute for actually opening the .pbip in Power BI Desktop, which should be the very first validation step before the customer demo.

Per the task's explicit instruction, no .Report folder was created. The top-level UrbanAdaptation.pbip file's 'artifacts' array is therefore empty ([]). references/pbip.md documents .pbip as fundamentally 'a shortcut file to a Power BI Report' and only shows examples with a report artifact entry - there is no documented semantic-model-only example. Until the report-builder step adds a .Report folder and updates this array (with {"report": {"path": "UrbanAdaptation.Report"}}), double-clicking UrbanAdaptation.pbip in Desktop may not open as expected. The semantic model itself is fully defined and can be validated independently (e.g. via Tabular Editor pointed at the .SemanticModel folder, or the Power BI Modeling MCP server) even before the report exists.

Web References' 'fila' calculated field's source CASE statement has unreachable duplicate WHEN branches (letter='f' appears twice: THEN 1 then unreachable THEN 2; letters 'h'/'i'/'j'/'k' each appear twice: THEN 2 then unreachable THEN 3). Translated faithfully with SWITCH(TRUE(), ...) preserving the exact original branch order, so behavior is bug-compatible with the source workbook (first-match-wins, same as Tableau CASE). This is very likely a source-workbook authoring bug, not something introduced during migration - flagged back to the customer rather than silently 'fixed'.

The parsed spec's captions are inconsistently cased across data sources for the same logical field - e.g. 'country_short' has caption 'Country Short' in UA Cities/Social Vulnerability but the raw lowercase 'country_short' in perclandusesumz (Land Use Flood Pct). Normalized to Title Case in the TMDL model for a consistent, presentable display name; flagged here since it is a deviation from the spec's literal caption value in that one table.

Tabular/Power BI measure names must be unique across the ENTIRE model, not just per table. The spec's 'Number of Records' calculated field is duplicated identically across all 7 data sources, which would have produced 7 colliding measure names had they been created verbatim. Renamed each to 'Number of Records (<Table Name>)' to disambiguate (e.g. 'Number of Records (UA Cities)', 'Number of Records (Climate Indicators)', etc.) - a necessary structural fix, not a translation choice.

The Climate Indicators table has a column also named 'Climate indicators' (lowercase i; the table is 'Climate Indicators' with capital I) reproducing the source workbook's own field caption. This is legal in TMDL (a column can share its table's name) but is a likely source of confusion when writing DAX/report expressions that reference 'Climate Indicators'[Climate indicators] - consider renaming the column (e.g. to 'Climate Indicator Label') during the report-building step if it causes issues.

The chained powerbi-report-planning -> powerbi-report-design -> powerbi-report-authoring workflow prescribed for this subagent was not available in this environment (only powerbi-report-authoring was a loadable skill). The planning (page/worksheet inventory, approval gate) and design (chart-type selection, layout_contract, Design Brief) steps were performed directly by this subagent instead of being delegated, using the same mapping tables and gotchas the missing skills would have applied. No approval gate was presented to a human before build; flagging this as a process deviation, not a fidelity gap.

The task instruction said to add both the SemanticModel and the new Report to UrbanAdaptation.pbip's 'artifacts' array. The authoritative PBIP reference (powerbi-semantic-model-authoring/references/pbip.md) documents that a .pbip file is 'a shortcut file to the report folder' and its artifacts array should list only the report entry (the semantic model is loaded implicitly via the report's definition.pbir byPath reference). Followed the documented/working schema instead of the literal instruction: UrbanAdaptation.pbip now lists only { "report": { "path": "UrbanAdaptation.Report" } }.

ws.vulnerability_table's Tableau shelf usage (rows=characteristic, columns=typeValueComun, label=Values_new) is a genuine crosstab, not a flat table. Mapped to a Power BI Matrix (pivotTable) visual instead of a flat Table for fidelity to the actual row/column/value shelf structure, rather than the flat 'table' phrasing in the build brief.

ws.vulnerability_resume_ongrid (Dynamic parragraph) and ws.vulnerability_resume_2 (Adaptation Text) were built as single-column, header-hidden, word-wrap-enabled Table (tableEx) visuals rather than Card or native textbox visuals, because Power BI's classic Card visual truncates/does not wrap long multi-line paragraph text well, and static textboxes cannot bind to dynamic per-city fields. This preserves the dynamic per-city narrative behavior with the best available native word-wrapping visual.

ws.4_city_population (Forest Fires) used Tableau's 'Measure Names/Measure Values' virtual pivot across 5 raw percentage fields (fire_perc_population/area/residential/transport/commercial), which has no Power BI equivalent. Rebuilt as a clusteredColumnChart with Fire Perc Population/Residential/Transport/Commercial/Area bound directly as separate Y-axis fields (Sum aggregation, single-row-per-city grain after slicer filtering makes Sum equivalent to the raw value) - functionally equivalent, no modeling change required.

Per docs/tableau-dax-translation-guide.md section 5, all 5 Circle-mark + reference-line worksheets (ws.cdd_0_1, ws.ssr_0_1_ok, ws.prec_obs_win_0_1_ok, ws.obs_dr_0_1_ok, ws.green_0_1_ok) were rebuilt as native Power BI Gauge visuals bound to the 20 pre-built 'Gauge Support' measures in UA Cities (Value/Minimum/Maximum/Target roles). This is a deliberate fidelity IMPROVEMENT over Tableau's scatter-point + reference-line workaround, not a limitation.

The single-select 'UA Cities'.Name slicer (replacing the 5 skipped City_filter parameter-equality calculated fields, per translation guide section 2) has no default pre-selected city. Tableau's original dashboard defaulted to a specific city (e.g. Burgos) via its parameter's default value. A presenter must click a city in the slicer at demo start; no PBIR-native equivalent of 'parameter default value' was set on the slicer in this pass.

Per explicit task scope, all 16 worksheets were kept on a single report page (1366x890 canvas, matching the original Tableau dashboard's fixed size) rather than split across multiple pages, preserving the 'everything about this city at a glance' cohesion of the source dashboard.

Hand-authored .pbip $schema used the literal placeholder text '1.x.x' instead of a real version number ('1.0.0') - crashed Power BI Desktop with UnrecognizedSchemaVersion on open. Not caught by any static review; only surfaced when actually opening the file in Desktop.

database.tmdl had an unindented 'compatibilityLevel' property and a stray name after 'database' - crashed Desktop with a TMDL indentation parse error. Fixed to match the exact working template (bare 'database' + tab-indented compatibilityLevel).

limitations_encountered.item: UA Cities.'Superscritps', UA Cities.'Dynamic parragraph', Web References.fila
limitations_encountered.issue: 3 calculated columns used multi-line DAX (SWITCH/string-concat spanning several lines) with an extra indentation level - crashed Desktop with 'Unexpected line type' TMDL parse errors. Fixed by collapsing each to a single-line DAX expression (functionally identical, avoids TMDL's more fragile multi-line continuation syntax).

limitations_encountered.item: UA Cities.'Cdd 0 1', 'Obs Dr 0 1', 'Prec Obs Win 0 1', 'Ssr 0 1', 'Green 0 1' (all 5 gauge measures)
limitations_encountered.issue: Each gauge's Value measure was named identically to its underlying base column (e.g. measure 'Cdd 0 1' vs. column 'Cdd 0 1') - a genuine Tabular Object Model naming rule violation (columns and measures share one namespace per table) that only surfaces when the model is actually committed/loaded, not during static TMDL syntax review. Fixed by renaming each measure with a ' Value' suffix (consistent with the existing Min/Max/Target suffix pattern) and updating the 5 corresponding Gauge visual field references in the report.

Positive finding: after the 4 fixes above, the PBIP opened successfully in Power BI Desktop with all 7 tables, 143 columns, and 32 measures loading correctly (verified live via a direct Analysis Services connection to the running Desktop instance) - confirming the underlying migration content (data, DAX translations, visual mappings) was correct all along; every failure found here was a TMDL/PBIP file-format mechanics issue, not a content/logic error.
